Curry Lentil & Vegetable Soup:
Directions
Dice the onion and carrot together. 1
Drain chickpeas 2
Wash, peel, and dice (1/2") russet potatoes 3
Heal oil in large pot over medium heat. Once oil is hot, toss in onions and carrots. Cook until tender and onions appear translucent, about 5 min. 4
Stir in curry powder and 1 tsp salt. Add lentils, potatoes, and 4 cups of broth. Stir to combine. 5
Bring mixture to a boil. Lower heat to medium and simmer covered for 20 min (or until lentils are tender). 6
Add chickpeas, lemon juice, and 1/2 cup water to food processor and puree until smooth. 7
When lentil mixture is finished simmering, remove from heat and stir in the chikpea mixture. 8
Add butter and roughly chopped spinach. Stir in and let the butter melt and spinach wilt for a minute or 2 in the hot soup. 9
Add 1 tsp garlic powder, 1 tsp black pepper. Add additional salt/pepper to taste. 10
Veggie Salad with Feta & Mayo:
Directions
Heat and cool fire-roasted corn. 1
Rinse and drain black beans. 2
Chop cucumbers, bell peppers, and shallots together. 3
Mix all ingredients (except corn chips and limes) together in a large mixing bowl. 4
Squeeze the juice from the limes onto the salad. Add desired amounts of chili powder, garlic powder, onion powder, black pepper. 5
Divide the salad and corn chips into meal portions. Don't add corn chips to salad until serving. 6
Butternut Squash Broccoli Cheddar Chicke:
Directions
Preheat oven to 350 F. 1
Cube the squash, then toss with 1 Tbsp olive oil, 1/2 Tbsp maple syrup, 1/2 teaspon chili powder, 1/2 teaspoon cayenne pepper, and generous amount of salt & pepper. 2
Line baking sheet with parchment paper, place squash cubes on it, and bake 20-30 min, flipping halfway through, until squash is tender. 3
Let squash cubes rest on baking sheet while preparing the rest of the recipe. 4
Cube the chicken, then add to skillet with 1 Tbsp olive oil over medium-high heat. Sprinkle with 1 tsp garlic powder, 1/2 tsp paprika, 1/2 tsp cayenne, and generous amount of salt & pepper. Cook 5-8 min, until no longer pink, then move to mixing bowl. 5
In this order, add the following to large pot: 1.75 cup water, 1 cup pearl couscous, 3.5 cups broccoli florets. Bring to a boil, then cover, reduce heat to low and cook for 8-10 minutes total, stirring halfway through. 6
Stir again, then add in cooked chicken and 1.5 cups shredded cheddar. Stir to melt the cheddar. 7
Fold in the roasted butternut squash. Taste and adjust seasonings as necessary. 8
Bean & Broccoli Salad:
Directions
Chop raw bacon and dill (separately). Drain beans. Slice scallions. 1
Cook chopped bacon in a large skillet over medium-high heat, stirring occasionally, until crisp, about 6 minutes. Transfer to a paper towel–lined plate. Measure 1 tablespoon of the drippings into mixing bowl 2
Add ⅓ cup yogurt, ⅓ cup mayonnaise, 2½ tablespoons vinegar, 2 tablespoons dill, 1 teaspoon garlic powder, 1 teaspoon onion powder and 1/2 teaspoon pepper to the bowl 3
Whisk until combined. Add 4 cups broccoli, the rinsed cannellini beans, 1 cup Cheddar and the cooked bacon and all but 2 tablespoons of the sliced scallions; toss until evenly coated. Top with the remaining 2 tablespoons scallions. 4
Calabacitas a la Mexicana:
Directions
Dice the 1/2 white onion. Separately, dice the jalapenos, squash, and tomatoes. 1
Heat the butter and oil in a large skillet over medium-low heat. Add the onion and cook until fragrant and translucent, about 6 minutes. 2
Add the jalapeños, squash, tomatoes and 2 tablespoons water. Stir in 4 tsp marjoram, 1 tsp garlic powder, and 2 tsp each of salt and pepper. 3
Cover and cook, adding a couple more tablespoons of water about halfway through if the squash seems to be sticking to the bottom of the pan, until the squash is tender, 17 to 20 minutes. 4
Add sour cream and queso fresco, then divide into servings. Serve with corn chips. 5
Mediterranean Spiced Millet & Vegetables:
Directions
Cook millet, broth, 1 tsp pepper, 1 tsp salt in a pot until liquid is entirely absorbed. 1
Saute onion and celery in olive oil until translucent. Add garlic and saute for 2 more min. 2
Slice and boil carrots in water until tender. 3
Add millet mixture to onions, celery and garlic. Add tomato puree, 1 tsp garlic powder, 1/2 tsp aleppo pepper, 1 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp pepper. Cook on med-low for approx 10 min. 4
Add carrots to the mixture and cook for a couple more min. 5
Serve and garnish with parsely, mint, and pepitas. 6
